metadata.title: Fractional Head of Sales for B2B founders | Accelr
metadata.description: Accelr helps B2B tech-founders go from ad-hoc sales to a scalable, AI-driven commercial system. Diagnosis, implementation and leadership.

hero.title: Your sales growth depends on you. That's the problem.
hero.subtitle: I build the sales foundation, strategy, process and team, so growth no longer depends on you alone. From diagnosis to operational sales leadership.
hero.ctaSubtext: 30 minutes · No obligations · You'll walk away with 3 concrete action points.

painPoints.label: / Sound familiar?
painPoints.title: Make room for growth.
painPoints.subtitle: If your sales growth depends on you as a founder, that's a structural problem. Do you recognize these situations?
items: Scaling requires a system, not more hours.

keuzeblok.label: / How we work together
keuzeblok.title: How do we work together?
keuzeblok.subtitle: Every engagement starts with diagnosis. Then we decide together how deeply I get involved.

cards.title: De Scan™
cards.subtitle: One-time diagnosis
cards.body: I analyze your complete sales operation: process, team, tooling and data. You receive a report with hard numbers, a priority list and a concrete roadmap.

cards.title: De Bouw
cards.subtitle: Specific building blocks
cards.body: I build specific components of your sales infrastructure. CRM setup, outbound engine, playbooks, AI automations, scoped and results-driven.
cards.details: Based on Scan results or your own brief

cards.title: Fractional Head of Sales
cards.subtitle: Part-time commercial leader
cards.body: I become your part-time commercial leader. 1-2 days per week, minimum 6 months. Includes diagnosis and everything that's needed.

keuzeblok.note: Not sure what you need? Start with De Scan. For a Fractional engagement, the diagnosis is included in the first month, you don't pay for De Scan separately.

formule.label: / The framework
formule.title: One system, four levers
formule.desc: Three pillars that reinforce each other. AI is the multiplier. If one pillar is missing, you're multiplying by zero.

bouwblokken.label: / Building blocks

bouwblokken.subtitle: These are the components I deploy, on a project basis or ongoing.

cards.title: AI & Automation
cards.desc: Make, n8n, Clay, automate what no human needs to do

bouwblokken.note: During De Scan, I analyze all building blocks. During De Bouw, I work on specific components. As Fractional Head of Sales, I oversee everything on an ongoing basis.

vergelijking.title: What does sales leadership cost?
vergelijking.footnote: 90 days = CRM set up, process documented, playbooks live. A predictable pipeline takes 6+ months. Sources: Robert Half Salary Guide NL 2024, Glassdoor Netherlands.

models.title: De Scan™
models.result: 47-point diagnosis, prioritized roadmap, scan report with hard numbers

models.title: Fractional Head of Sales

overTim.label: / The team
overTim.title: The person behind Accelr
overTim.name: Tim Samsom
overTim.bio: 8+ years B2B sales in adtech and tech. As Country Manager Netherlands at ShowHeroes, I built the Dutch operation from scratch, from first client to a structural commercial team. Previously responsible for sales at Streamads, and commercial roles at Ematters and XS4ALL. Nyenrode Business University alumnus.
overTim.quote: Most sales problems are not people problems. They're system problems.

voorWie.label: / Who it's for
voorWie.title: Is this for you?
voorJou: B2B SaaS, tech or IT services company
€1M-€10M revenue, 10-80 employees
Technical founder who is (still) selling personally
Just made a first sales hire, or the first one failed
Looking for a system, not a consultant
nietVoorJouItems: Looking for someone to "take over" without your involvement
Working team of 10+ (you need a full-time HoS)

items.q: What is a Fractional Head of Sales?
items.a: A Fractional Head of Sales is an experienced commercial leader who works 1-2 days per week to build your sales organization. You get strategic leadership and hands-on execution without the cost of a full-time hire (€150K+/year). This model has been mainstream in the US for years; in the Netherlands, it's just getting started.

Of the founders I speak with, 80% think the problem is "more leads." After De Scan, it almost always turns out to be a process, CRM or qualification issue. De Scan prevents you from spending money on the wrong solution. Disagree with the findings? You get your money back.

items.q: How does Fractional differ from Interim?
items.a: An interim manager fills a gap and keeps operations running. A Fractional Head of Sales focuses on acceleration: building, team development and establishing structure. It costs 60-70% less than a full-time hire, and the goal is to build a foundation so you can scale.

items.q: How quickly will I see results?
items.a: De Scan delivers a clear picture within 2-3 weeks. With a Build project, the first components are operational within 4-6 weeks. With a Fractional engagement, you'll have a configured CRM and documented process after 90 days, pipeline impact after 3-4 months, and structural growth after 6+ months. Honestly: lasting results take time.

ctaFooter.title: Ready to professionalize your sales?
ctaFooter.scanNote: Or start with De Scan, €3,500 fixed, money-back guarantee.

metadata.title: The Accelr Method™ | Diagnosis → Build → Run
metadata.description: From ad-hoc sales to a scalable system. 10 dimensions, 4 phases, working systems that belong to you.

hero.label: / The Accelr Method™
hero.title: The Accelr Method™
hero.subtitle: Three pillars, one multiplier. One system that keeps your sales engine running, even when you're not there.

intro.label: / Method
intro.title: Every B2B company needs a commercial system.
intro.p1: Every B2B company that wants to grow beyond founder-led sales needs a commercial system. Not standard advice. Not a report that ends up in a drawer. A working system built on three pillars that reinforce each other, powered by AI.
intro.p2: De Scan diagnoses where it leaks. The formula determines what I build.

formule.label: The formula
formule.desc: Strategy is the input that drives everything. Process and People are the core pillars, they reinforce each other multiplicatively. AI is the multiplier that accelerates everything. Improve each element by 20% and your total output grows by 107%.

cards.title: De Scan
cards.desc: All pillars are analyzed. You receive a score and priority list per pillar.

cards.title: De Bouw
cards.desc: You choose specific pillars to work on. Project-based, scoped.

cards.title: Fractional Head of Sales
cards.desc: All pillars, ongoing, with me as the owner. Continuous optimization.

items.title: Sales Maturity Score across 10 dimensions
items.desc: A complete audit of your sales operation. No opinions, measurable scores per dimension with concrete improvement points.

items.title: Prioritized action plan with euro value
items.desc: Every opportunity gets an estimated impact in euros, a time estimate and a priority. You know exactly where to start.

items.title: Working systems that belong to you
items.desc: Everything I build, CRM, automations, playbooks, campaigns, is fully yours. No lock-in, no dependency, no license fees.

voorWie.title: Who it's for
voorWie.desc1: For founders and commercial directors who built the company on their own sales talent. Where every deal goes through the same person. Where there's no CRM, or one that nobody uses. Where growth means: working harder, not smarter.
voorWie.desc2: The Accelr Method brings structure where there's chaos, predictability where chance rules, and a system that's scalable without you having to close every deal yourself.
profielItems: B2B SaaS, tech or IT services

steps.title: Week 1-2: Scanning
steps.desc: I start with a 60-minute kickoff to map goals, systems and constraints. Then in-depth interviews with founder, sales and marketing follow. I audit your CRM (or lack thereof), analyze your pipeline, check your tech stack and assess your AI readiness.
steps.resultDesc: Sales Maturity Score across 10 dimensions. A clear picture of where you stand, and where the money is.
steps.price: €3,500 fixed · Not satisfied? Money back. No conditions.

steps.title: Week 2: Planning
steps.desc1: Based on the Scan, I create an implementation plan. Which systems need to be built, in what order, and what each step delivers. Fixed price per component, agreed upfront. No surprises.
steps.desc2: The plan integrates with your existing tools and is structured so that I can build it, or you can pick it up yourself.

steps.title: Week 3-10: Building
steps.desc: I build what the plan calls for. No standard packages, only what you need.
steps.overdracht: Everything is delivered working, documented and handed over. The system is yours.

steps.title: Ongoing: Running & Scaling
steps.desc: A system without leadership deteriorates within 3 months. I stay on as your Fractional Head of Sales, not advising, but managing.
items: First sales hire: role definition, onboarding, coaching
steps.details: 1-2 days per week · Min. 3 months · Monthly cancellable thereafter
steps.note: The goal is that you no longer need me. That's not failure, that's the plan.

ctaFooter.title: Ready to build your sales engine?
ctaFooter.scanNote: €3,500 fixed · Money-back guarantee · Results in 2 weeks

metadata.title: De Scan™ | Sales Operation Diagnosis | Accelr
metadata.description: Sales diagnosis in 2 weeks. Score across 10 dimensions, opportunities with euro value, action plan. €3,500 fixed. Not satisfied? Money back.

hero.label: / Always step 1
hero.title: De Scan: know where you're leaving money on the table,
hero.subtitle: A professional audit of your sales operation. In 1-2 weeks, I map everything out and deliver an action plan with prioritized opportunities and euro value.

probleem.label: / The problem
probleem.title: Most founders know something is wrong with their sales. They just don't know
probleem.desc1: You close deals, but it's not repeatable. You have tools, but nobody uses them well. You know you need structure, but where do you start?
probleem.desc2: De Scan provides the answer. Not with opinions, with data.

dimensies.label: / 10 Dimensions
dimensies.title: I audit your sales operation across 10 dimensions
dimensies.subtitle: Each dimension scores 1-10.

items.name: Strategy
items.question: How sharp is your ICP? Does your positioning hold up?

items.name: Process
items.question: Do you have a documented sales process? Is it being followed?

items.name: CRM
items.question: Do you use a CRM? How is your data quality?

items.name: Outbound
items.question: Do you do active outbound? With what results?

items.name: Enablement
items.question: Does your team have playbooks, scripts, training?

items.name: AI & Automation
items.question: What tools do you use? What can be automated?

items.name: Team
items.question: Do you have the right people in the right roles?

items.name: Data
items.question: Which KPIs do you track? How accurate is your forecast?

items.name: Leadership
items.question: Who manages sales? Is there accountability?

deliverables.label: / Deliverables
deliverables.title: What you get
items: Sales Maturity Score, score across 10 dimensions (1-10)
CRM & pipeline audit (or gap analysis if there's no CRM)
Top 3-5 opportunities with estimated euro value
Recommendation per opportunity: DIY / with Accelr / outsource
Live presentation of findings (never just by email)

week1.label: / Week 1
items: Interviews with founder, sales and marketing

week2.label: / Week 2
items: Identifying opportunities and estimating euro value

garantie.title: My guarantee
garantie.headline: Not satisfied? Money back. No conditions.
garantie.desc: I can guarantee this because in 8+ years I've never seen a company where there's nothing to improve in the sales process.

waaromBetaald.label: / Honest
waaromBetaald.title: Why paid?
waaromBetaald.desc: Free diagnostics = conflict of interest. A doctor charges for an MRI. An accountant charges for an audit. I charge for a professional diagnosis. The deliverable has standalone value, you can immediately take action, even without a follow-up engagement.

items.q: Can I just do De Scan without any follow-up?
items.a: Yes. De Scan delivers an action plan you can immediately work with on your own. No mandatory follow-up, no commitment.

items.q: What if I already have a CRM?
items.a: Then I audit your current setup: data quality, pipeline configuration, reporting, and team adoption. Having a CRM and using a CRM well are two different things.
At minimum: access to your CRM (if you have one), and availability for a 60-90 minute interview. I handle the rest.

metadata.title: About Tim Samsom | Fractional Head of Sales | Accelr
metadata.description: 8+ years B2B sales in adtech and tech. Country Manager at ShowHeroes, now Fractional Head of Sales. Nyenrode alumnus.

hero.label: / About Tim
hero.title: The person behind the sales engine.
hero.subtitle: I combine the strategic perspective of a commercial director with the hands-on approach of an implementation specialist. And I use AI as a tool, not a gimmick.

verhaal.label: / My story
verhaal.title: From scale-up to Accelr
paragraphs: Before I started Accelr, I was Country Manager Netherlands at ShowHeroes, a European adtech scale-up. There I built the Dutch operation from scratch: from first client to a profitable market with a structural commercial team.
Before that, I worked 6+ years in B2B sales at Streamads, Ematters and XS4ALL. Nyenrode Business University alumnus.
learnings: Setting up sales processes where there were none
Closing deals from €10K to €500K+ with enterprise clients
Managing teams and building commercial operations
The reality that 80% of sales success comes from the system, not the talent
verhaal.patroon: What I saw time and time again with tech founders: brilliant products, but no sales engine. The founder does all the sales themselves, there's no process, no CRM, no strategy. Growth depends on chance and network.

cards.title: Diagnosis first
cards.text: I only build once I know what you need. Every collaboration starts with De Scan.

cards.title: I build it myself

cards.title: AI as a tool

cards.title: No dependency

metadata.title: Sales Strategy & GTM for B2B | Accelr
metadata.description: ICP, go-to-market plan, positioning and pricing. The foundation that ensures all your sales activities work in the right direction.

hero.label: / Sales Strategy & GTM
hero.title: Knowing who you're selling to is half the battle.
hero.subtitle: ICP, go-to-market plan, positioning and pricing. The foundation that ensures all your sales activities work in the right direction.

probleem.title: Most B2B companies sell to everyone.
paragraphs: No sharp ICP. No clear positioning. Pricing that was "decided once" and never revisited. And then expecting that outbound, content and sales calls will convert.
The result: your team spends 80% of their time on prospects who will never become customers. Your messaging is generic. Your pipeline is full of deals that don't move.
Sales strategy isn't a luxury, it's the foundation. Without sharp targeting and positioning, everything you build after that is less effective.

items.title: ICP & Buyer Persona
items.desc: Who are your best-fit customers? Which companies, which roles, which pain points? No vague persona posters, a sharp profile that your targeting, messaging and entire sales process is built on.

items.title: Go-to-Market Plan
items.desc: Which market segments do you tackle first? Through which channels? With what message? A concrete plan that sets priorities and makes clear where you do and don't invest.

items.title: Positioning & Messaging
items.desc: How do you position yourself relative to alternatives? What makes your offering relevant to your ICP? Messaging frameworks for your website, outbound, sales calls and proposals.

items.title: Pricing & Deal Structure
items.desc: What do you charge, how do you present it, and how do you structure deals? Pricing that fits your market, your value proposition and your sales process.

items.title: Analysis
items.desc: Audit current clients, market and competition

items.title: Sharpen ICP
items.desc: Define best-fit profile based on data

items.title: Build strategy
items.desc: GTM plan, positioning, pricing

items.title: Activate
items.desc: Translate into campaigns, content and sales

voorJou: You're selling to 'everyone' and closing too few deals
You don't have a clear ICP or buyer persona
Your messaging doesn't resonate with your target audience
You don't know which market segments to prioritize

faq.a: No. Everything I deliver is immediately actionable: targeting lists, messaging templates, a GTM plan with concrete actions. No PowerPoint strategy.
Typically 2-3 weeks from start to delivery, depending on scope. De Scan determines what's needed.

faq.q: What if I already have an ICP?
faq.a: Then we validate it. In my experience, most companies' ICPs are wrong or too broad. We sharpen it based on data.

cta.title: First the foundation. Then build.
cta.desc: De Scan maps out whether your strategy holds up, and where the biggest opportunities are to sell more effectively.

metadata.title: CRM Implementation for SMBs & Scale-ups | Accelr
metadata.description: CRM selection, setup, pipeline design and dashboards. From spreadsheet to working system in 3-4 weeks.

hero.label: / CRM & Pipeline
hero.title: A CRM your team actually uses.
hero.subtitle: Not yet another tool that becomes an expensive database after 3 months. A working system with pipeline, dashboards and reporting.

probleem.title: 70% of CRM implementations fail.
paragraphs: Not because the CRM is bad. But because nobody sets it up properly. The pipeline doesn't make sense. The fields are confusing. The dashboards show the wrong data. And after three months, your team works around it instead of in it.
The result: you have a CRM that costs €500/month and a spreadsheet you still actually use.
I set up your CRM so it works for how your team sells, not the other way around.

items.title: CRM Selection
items.desc: HubSpot, Pipedrive or Salesforce, depending on your stage, budget and complexity. Independent advice, no partnerships.

items.title: Pipeline Design
items.desc: Stages that fit your sales process. Lead scoring, deal stages, automatic reminders. No standard template.

items.title: Dashboards & Reporting
items.desc: Real-time overview of pipeline, conversion rates, forecast and activity per rep. Data you can actually act on.

items.title: Integrations
items.desc: Your CRM connected to your email, calendar, outbound tools and marketing automation. One source of truth.

items.title: Audit
items.desc: Audit current CRM (or lack thereof)

items.title: Design
items.desc: Pipeline, fields, automations, dashboards

items.title: Migration
items.desc: Transfer and clean up data

items.title: Training
items.desc: Train the team on the new system

voorJou: You don't have a CRM or still work in spreadsheets
You have a CRM but nobody uses it properly
You can't predict your pipeline
You lack reporting to manage by

faq.a: Depends on your stage. HubSpot for most SMBs, Pipedrive if you want to start lean, Salesforce for more complex sales cycles. De Scan provides a substantiated recommendation.

faq.q: How long does a CRM implementation take?
faq.a: Typically 3-4 weeks from start to working system, including data migration and team training.

faq.q: What if we already have a CRM?
faq.a: Then I optimize what you have. Often it's not the CRM that's the problem, but the setup.

cta.title: From spreadsheet to working system.
cta.desc: De Scan maps out what you need: CRM selection, setup, or optimization of what you already have.

metadata.title: Outbound Lead Generation for B2B | Accelr
metadata.description: Outbound email, LinkedIn outreach and multi-channel sequences. Predictable pipeline without depending on your network.

hero.label: / Outbound & Lead Generation
hero.title: A pipeline that doesn't depend on your network.
hero.subtitle: Outbound email, LinkedIn and multi-channel sequences. Automated, tested, and working.

probleem.title: Network-based sales has a ceiling.
paragraphs: The first customers came through your network. Referrals. An intro via a former colleague. That worked, until it wasn't enough anymore.
Now you need predictable inflow. But cold outbound feels like spam, LinkedIn outreach delivers nothing, and one tool after another promises "10 meetings per week" with no results.
The problem isn't outbound itself. The problem is that nobody sets it up properly: right targeting, relevant messaging, multi-channel sequencing, and continuous optimization.

items.title: Outbound Email Engine
items.desc: Instantly or Lemlist, fully configured. Domain warmup, DKIM/SPF/DMARC, inbox rotation. Deliverability from day one.

items.title: LinkedIn Outreach
items.desc: Connection strategy, message sequences, content alignment. Manual or automated, depending on your preference.

items.title: Multi-Channel Sequences
items.desc: Email + LinkedIn + phone in one coordinated flow. Each step builds on the previous one. No disconnected channels.

items.title: Targeting & Messaging
items.desc: ICP-based lists via Clay/Apollo. Messaging that resonates with your buyers, tested, optimized, AI-personalized.

items.title: Sharpen ICP
items.desc: Who are your best-fit customers, really?

items.title: Set up infra
items.desc: Domains, tooling, warmup

items.title: Build campaigns
items.desc: Lists, messaging, sequences

items.title: Test & optimize
items.desc: A/B tests, response tracking, iterate

voorJou: You depend on your network for new clients
You've tried outbound but it delivered nothing
You want a predictable inflow of qualified leads
You don't have time to build and test campaigns yourself

faq.q: How many meetings can I expect?
faq.a: Depends on your market and offering. Typically 5-15 qualified meetings per month after the first optimization round. No empty promises, I give a realistic estimate after De Scan.
Poorly executed outbound is spam. Well-executed outbound is offering relevant value to people who would benefit from it. The difference is in targeting and messaging.

faq.q: How quickly is it up and running?
faq.a: Domain warmup takes 2-3 weeks. First results within 4-6 weeks. Optimization is ongoing.

cta.title: From network-dependent to predictable pipeline.
cta.desc: De Scan maps out which outbound approach delivers the most for your market and offering.

metadata.title: Sales Enablement for B2B Teams | Accelr
metadata.description: Playbooks, discovery frameworks, objection handling and team training. Give your sales team the structure and tools to perform consistently.

hero.label: / Sales Enablement
hero.title: A sales team that knows what to do.
hero.subtitle: Playbooks, discovery frameworks, objection handling and training. Structure that ensures your team performs consistently.

probleem.title: Your team sells on gut feeling.
paragraphs: Every rep does it differently. Discovery is a checklist. Objections are improvised. New hires take months to get up to speed.
The problem isn't talent, it's lack of structure. Without a playbook, without a framework, without a shared approach, every deal is an individual experiment.
Sales enablement gives your team the tools, knowledge and structure to perform consistently. Not as a one-time training, but as a working system.

items.title: Sales Playbook
items.desc: The complete playbook for your sales process. From first contact to close. Steps, templates, scripts and best practices, so new reps get productive faster and experienced reps perform more consistently.

items.title: Discovery Framework
items.desc: Structured discovery that surfaces the right information. No checklist conversations, but a framework that helps identify real pain points and buying readiness.

items.title: Objection Handling
items.desc: The 15-20 most common objections in your market, with substantiated responses. Tested, trained and ready for daily use by your team.

items.title: Training & Coaching
items.desc: Hands-on training for your team on the new tools and frameworks. Not a one-time workshop, a training program that sticks.

items.title: Analysis
items.desc: Map current sales process and bottlenecks

items.title: Develop
items.desc: Build playbook, frameworks and materials

items.title: Train
items.desc: Train team on new approach

items.title: Embed
items.desc: Anchor in daily practice and CRM

voorJou: Your sales team performs inconsistently
New reps take too long to become productive
There's no standardized sales process
You're losing deals due to poor discovery or follow-up

faq.q: Does this also work for small teams (2-3 reps)?
faq.a: Especially. With small teams, consistency is extra important. A playbook ensures everyone sells the same way, and that knowledge isn't lost when someone leaves.

faq.q: How is this different from a standard sales training?
faq.a: I don't build generic training. Everything is based on your market, your product and your sales process. The result is a working system, not a binder of slides.

faq.q: How long before it has an effect?
faq.a: Most teams see improvement in discovery quality and conversion rates within 4-6 weeks. Full adoption typically takes 2-3 months.

cta.title: Give your team the structure to win.
cta.desc: De Scan maps out where your sales process breaks down and which enablement components have the most impact.

metadata.title: AI Sales Automation for B2B | Accelr
metadata.description: AI structurally integrated into your sales process. Clay, Make, n8n, Apollo. No experiments, working systems.

hero.label: / AI & Automation
hero.title: AI that actually sells.
hero.subtitle: No ChatGPT experiments. Structural AI integration into your sales process that measurably generates more pipeline and revenue.

probleem.title: Most companies use AI as a toy.
paragraphs: A prompt in ChatGPT. A list from Apollo. An automation that runs once and then breaks.

The problem isn't the technology, it's the implementation. AI only works when it's structurally embedded in your sales process: from prospecting to personalization to follow-up. Not as a standalone tool, but as part of a working system.
That's what I build. AI-driven sales infrastructure that runs without you having to write every prompt yourself.

items.title: AI Prospecting Engine
items.desc: Automatically find and enrich your ideal customers with Clay and Apollo. Company data, contact details, technographics and intent signals, without manual searching.

items.title: Workflow Automation
items.desc: Make and n8n workflows that connect your CRM, email tools and prospecting. Incoming leads are automatically enriched, scored and routed.

items.title: AI Personalization
items.desc: Outbound messages that don't read like spam. AI-generated personalization based on company data, LinkedIn activity and tech stack. At scale, in your tone of voice.

items.title: Prompt Libraries & Templates
items.desc: A library of tested prompts for your team: discovery questions, email variants, objection handling, lead research. Ready for daily use.

items.title: Audit current stack
items.desc: What do you have, what's missing, what isn't working?

items.title: Architecture design
items.desc: Which tools, which flows, which triggers

items.title: Build & test
items.desc: I configure, integrate and test everything

items.title: Training & handover
items.desc: Your team can run it themselves

voorJou: You've bought tools but nobody makes them work
You want to use AI but don't know where to start
Your prospecting is manual and takes too much time
Your outbound messages are generic and don't convert

faq.q: Do I need to already have AI tools?
faq.a: No. I select and implement the right tools for your situation. De Scan maps out what you need.
Depends on scope. Typically €3,000-€8,000 as part of a larger engagement. De Scan determines the exact scope and price.

faq.q: Can my team maintain this themselves?
faq.a: Yes, that's the goal. I build it, document it, and train your team. The system is yours.

watIsHet.label: / What is it?
watIsHet.title: What is a Fractional Head of Sales?
watIsHet.desc: A Fractional Head of Sales is an experienced commercial leader who works part-time (1-2 days per week) for your company. You get strategic leadership and hands-on execution, from CRM setup to team coaching to pipeline management. In the US, this model has been common for years at over 120,000 companies. In the Netherlands, it's just starting.

vergelijking.label: / Comparison
vergelijking.title: What does sales leadership cost?
vergelijking.footnote: Interim keeps things running. Fractional brings acceleration, experience and direction.

steps.title: Running

metadata.title: Results | Case Studies | Accelr
metadata.description: No theory. This is what happens when your sales operation becomes a system.

hero.title: Results
hero.subtitle: No theory. This is what happens when your sales operation becomes a system.

caseStudyA.name: [Company A]
caseStudyA.headline: €1.8M → €2.6M ARR (+44%) in 9 months
highlights: Win rate +55%, deal size +33%, sales cycle -42%
HubSpot restructured, 6-step sales process, outbound engine built
Founder from 70% to 20% involvement in deals
caseStudyA.quote: After 6 months, it ran without me. Tim transformed our sales process from chaos to a system.
caseStudyA.uitdagingDesc: The founder did 70% of all deals himself. Two account executives worked without a playbook, without structured CRM usage, and without a defined sales process. Pipeline overview consisted of a Google Sheet. Win rate was unknown. The forecast was the founder's gut feeling.
caseStudyA.opdracht: Assignment: Fractional Head of Sales, 2 days per week, 9 months.
maand1Items: HubSpot audit: 40% of deals not logged
maand2Items: HubSpot restructured: pipeline stages, deal rotation, dashboards
Outbound engine built: Clay + Lemlist, 200 prospects per month
maand5Items: AI automations: lead scoring, Fireflies → CRM integration
caseStudyA.quoteDetailed: Tim forced us to honestly look at our pipeline. Confronting, 40% of deals weren't even in the CRM. After 3 months, we had real visibility into our funnel for the first time. After 6 months, it ran without me.

caseStudyB.name: [Company B]
caseStudyB.headline: €600K untapped potential found, Upsell +262%
highlights: Salesforce adoption from 15% to 92%, forecast accuracy 78%
New business engine: €0 to €220K per month
Client portfolio analysis revealed €600K hidden opportunities
caseStudyB.quote: The wake-up call we needed. We were sitting on €600K in potential we didn't see.
caseStudyB.uitdagingDesc: An IT services company with €3.2M revenue and 35 employees. Sales ran entirely on existing clients and inbound. No active new business. Salesforce had been purchased but adoption was at 15%. No pipeline overview, no forecasting, no structured upsell. Management knew there was more potential, but not how much.
scanItems: Salesforce audit: 85% of accounts incomplete, no opportunity tracking
Client portfolio analysis: €600K untapped cross-sell and upsell potential identified
No defined sales process, everything ad-hoc
Team assessment: 3 account managers, no pipeline ownership
bouwenItems: Salesforce restructured: pipeline stages, dashboards, deal tracking
Upsell playbook developed per client segment
New business outbound: LinkedIn + email sequences
caseStudyB.quoteDetailed: The wake-up call we needed. We were sitting on €600K in potential we didn't see. Tim made it visible and built a system to harvest it.

caseStudyC.name: [Company C]
caseStudyC.headline: €2.5M → €4.1M ARR (+64%) in 12 months
highlights: Full HubSpot implementation, AI lead scoring, outbound engine
Full-time Head of Sales recruited and successfully onboarded
caseStudyC.quote: The problem wasn't the person, it was the system. Tim built the foundation first.
caseStudyC.uitdagingDesc: A B2B SaaS company in supply chain with €2.5M ARR. The founder had just hired a VP Sales who left after 4 months, €120K loss including opportunity cost. The team of 3 AEs operated without a system. No CRM discipline, no forecasting, no structured sales process. The founder was considering a second hire but wanted to fix the foundation first.
maand1Items: CRM audit: Pipedrive → HubSpot migration necessary
maand5Items: Weekly pipeline reviews and quarterly business reviews
maand10Items: Full-time Head of Sales recruited and onboarded
caseStudyC.quoteDetailed: The problem wasn't the person, it was the system. Tim built the foundation first and then found the right HoS to take it over. I could never have done that myself.

metadata.title: Sales Maturity Score | Check your sales engine
metadata.description: 10 dimensions. One score. Know where your sales engine stalls and what the logical next step is.

form.label: / Sales Maturity Score
form.title: Where does your sales engine stand?
form.subtitle: Score yourself across 10 dimensions. You'll get immediate insight into your strengths and areas for improvement.

dimensions.name: Strategy
dimensions.question: How sharp is your ICP and positioning?
dimensions.low: Your ICP is vague or too broad. You're trying to sell to everyone.
dimensions.high: Sharp ICP, clear positioning, proven product-market fit.

dimensions.name: Process
dimensions.question: Do you have a documented sales process?
dimensions.low: No fixed process. Every deal goes differently.
dimensions.high: Documented process with clear stages and criteria.

dimensions.name: Pipeline
dimensions.question: How predictable is your pipeline?
dimensions.low: Four deals one month, zero the next.
dimensions.high: Predictable pipeline with consistent flow and accurate forecast.

dimensions.name: CRM
dimensions.question: How well is your CRM being used?
dimensions.low: No CRM, or a CRM that nobody uses.
dimensions.high: CRM fully adopted, clean data, usable reporting.

dimensions.name: Outbound
dimensions.question: Do you do active and systematic outbound?
dimensions.low: Inbound or network only. No systematic acquisition.

dimensions.name: Enablement
dimensions.question: Does your team have playbooks and training?
dimensions.high: Complete playbooks, trained team, fast onboarding.

dimensions.name: AI & Automation
dimensions.question: How far along are you with AI and automation?
dimensions.low: No AI tools, or tools bought but not working.
dimensions.high: AI structurally integrated across the entire sales process.

dimensions.name: Team
dimensions.question: Do you have the right people and structure?
dimensions.low: Founder does everything, or team without clear roles.
dimensions.high: Structured team with clear roles and responsibilities.

dimensions.name: Data
dimensions.question: Do you track the right KPIs?
dimensions.low: No KPI tracking. Decisions made on gut feeling.
dimensions.high: Full KPI tracking, accurate forecasts, data-driven decisions.

dimensions.name: Leadership
dimensions.question: Who manages the sales?
dimensions.high: Active sales management with reviews, coaching and accountability.

urgent.label: Urgent
urgent.desc: Your sales engine has fundamental gaps. You're leaving significant money on the table.

opbouwfase.label: Building phase
opbouwfase.desc: There are basic elements but the system lacks structure and consistency.

groeipotentieel.label: Growth potential
groeipotentieel.desc: Good foundation, but there's room for optimization and scaling.

optimalisatie.label: Optimization
optimalisatie.desc: Strong foundation. Focus on fine-tuning and maximizing efficiency.

urgent.label: Urgent
urgent.desc: You're leaving money on the table

metadata.title: Why your first sales hire fails (and how to prevent it) | Accelr Blog
metadata.description: Wasted €100K+ on a sales hire that doesn't work? This is why it goes wrong, and what you need to build first.

hero.title: Why your first sales hire fails

blogSalesHire.intro: You have product-market fit. Revenue is growing. Time for that first sales hire. But 6 months later: no results, €100K+ spent, and you're back to selling yourself. I see this pattern in 80% of the founders I speak with.

pattern.title: The pattern
pattern.p1: You have product-market fit. Revenue is growing. You think:
pattern.p2: You hire someone. After 3-6 months: no results.

items.rest: , "Anyone who pays is a good customer"

pattern.conclusion: The sales hire doesn't fail due to lack of talent, but due to lack of system.

whatToBuild.title: What you need to build first
whatToBuild.intro: Before making that first sales hire, you need this in place:

items.title: 1. Defined ICP and buyer persona
items.desc: Who are your best customers? Which titles? Which company sizes? Which pain points?

items.title: 2. CRM set up with pipeline stages
items.desc: What steps does a deal go through? From first contact to closed-won. What is the definition of each stage?

items.title: 3. Discovery and demo scripts
items.desc: What questions do you ask? What do you show in your demo? How do you handle objections?

items.title: 4. Onboarding checklist
items.desc: What does someone need to learn in week 1, 2, 3, 4? Which tools? Which processes?

items.title: 5. First 3 months KPIs
items.desc: How many calls? How many demos? What is success in month 1, 2, 3?

solution.title: The solution
solution.p1: Build the system first, then the hire.
solution.p2: If you can't or don't want to do that yourself: let a Fractional Head of Sales lay the foundation. In 3-6 months you build a system where any sales hire can be successful.
solution.p3: Then you're not hiring a person who needs to figure it out. You're hiring someone who can execute a proven playbook.

inlineCta.title: Want to know where you stand?
inlineCta.desc: With De Scan you get a complete overview of your sales funnel, tooling and processes in 2 hours. Plus a roadmap for what you need to fix before making that first hire.

footerCta.title: Want to prevent this?
footerCta.desc: Book a call. We'll look at your situation together and make a plan.

metadata.title: Fractional Head of Sales: what is it and why is it growing in the Netherlands? | Accelr Blog
metadata.description: Everything about the Fractional Head of Sales model. What it is, what it costs, and why more and more B2B companies in the Netherlands are considering it.

hero.title: Fractional Head of Sales: what is it?

blogFractional.intro: In the US, it's been mainstream for years. In the Netherlands, it's just starting. A Fractional Head of Sales is an experienced commercial leader who works part-time (1-2 days per week) for your company. This article explains what it is, what it costs, and when it's smart.

whatIsIt.title: What is a Fractional Head of Sales?
whatIsIt.p1: A Fractional Head of Sales is an experienced sales leader who works part-time (usually 1-2 days per week) to build and lead your commercial strategy and organization.
whatIsIt.p2: Compare it to a fractional CFO or CMO: senior expertise, without the cost of a full-time hire.
whatIsIt.p3: In the US, more than 120,000 companies work with fractional executives. In Europe, the model is growing fast, especially at B2B tech companies between €1M-€10M revenue.
whatIsIt.boxTitle: What does a Fractional Head of Sales do?
boxItems: Builds your sales funnel and CRM process
Trains your team or first sales hire
Helps with hiring and onboarding sales talent

cost.title: What does it cost?

options.title: Interim sales manager
options.desc: 5 days per week, often short-term focus

cost.conclusion: Fractional is **60-70% cheaper** than full-time or interim, but you still get senior-level expertise.

whenSmart.title: When is it smart?
whenSmart.intro: A Fractional Head of Sales is smart if:
items: Your founder sells personally and wants to **systematize** it
You've just made your **first sales hire** (or that one failed)
You have **no sales system**: no CRM, no playbook, no pipeline
You want to **grow to €5M-€20M** but don't know how

whenNot.title: When is it NOT smart?
whenNot.intro: Fractional is not the right choice if:
items: You're under **€500K revenue**, focus on product-market fit first
You already have a **sales team of 5+**, then you need full-time leadership
You want someone who **will also sell**, fractional is strategic, not execution
You're not willing to **delegate authority**, a fractional needs to be able to decide

howAccelr.title: How does it work at Accelr?
howAccelr.intro: At Accelr, I work in phases:

phases.title: Month 1: Diagnosis
phases.desc: We do De Scan. I map your funnel, tooling, ICP and processes. You get a roadmap with priorities.

phases.title: Month 2-3: Building
phases.desc: We build the foundation: CRM, playbook, scripts, ICP, onboarding, KPI dashboard. Everything you need to scale.

phases.title: Month 4+: Running & Optimizing
phases.desc: We train your team, manage KPIs, optimize the funnel. I stay available 1-2 days per week for coaching, strategy and decisions.

howAccelr.duration: Minimum duration: **6 months**. After that you can scale up to full-time, scale down, or continue with fractional, depending on what you need.

inlineCta.title: Is this for you?
inlineCta.desc: Let's find out. Book a call, I'll ask some questions about your situation, and we'll see if fractional fits.

footerCta.title: Want to explore this?
footerCta.desc: Book a call. We'll look together at whether fractional fits your situation.
